vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

generated_parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
generated_parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

verify.rule: All required fields are present and non-empty.
Mass estimate is a numeric value and within expected range.
All P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
All interfaces have reciprocal naming and joint evidence.
All critical joints have visible locking evidence and preload intent.
Boundary conditions are defined with fixture points, thermal zones, and pressure faces.

risk.desc: Thermal management channel may not provide sufficient cooling for high-altitude conditions.
risk.mit: Increase the cross-sectional area of the thermal management channel or add additional cooling paths.

risk.desc: The wing spar socket may not be able to handle the full load from the wing spar.
risk.mit: Review and potentially increase the dimensions of the wing spar socket to ensure it can handle the load.

risk.desc: The landing gear mount may not be robust enough for the landing loads.
risk.mit: Increase the thickness of the landing gear mount and add additional reinforcement.

risk.desc: The GPS boom mount may not be securely attached to the fuselage spine.
risk.mit: Add additional fasteners or a locking mechanism to the GPS boom mount.

risk.desc: The service access panel may not provide adequate access for maintenance.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the service access panel or add additional access points.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Nacelle loads from wing spar socket into fuselage spine
Avionics bay cooling flow to external environment
Thermal management from avionics bay to external cooling
Ruddervator motion transmitted to fuselage spine
External atmospheric pressure on fuselage structure
Electrical/data connection from avionics bay to external modules
Human/service access for maintenance and inspection

internal_feature_targets.description: Main structural beam of the center fuselage
Mounting point for the landing gear

Access point for maintenance and service

integration_opportunities.description: Integration of wing spar and landing gear mounts
Electrical routing through the avionics bay
Thermal management through the fuselage spine

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for FDM, orientation for CNC

verification_focus.description: Verification of structural integrity under flight loads
Verification of thermal management under high-altitude conditions
Verification of electrical connections and routing
Verification of service access points for maintenance

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high-altitude operation.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management systems to dissipate heat.

risk.desc: Risk of service access being obstructed by complex assembly.
risk.mit: Ensure clear pathways for maintenance and inspection.

risk.desc: Risk of structural integrity due to thin-air optimized props.

risk.desc: Risk of improper fastener torque leading to failure.

risk.desc: Risk of interface misalignment during assembly.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

physics_paths.description: Transfers rotor thrust and servo loads from nacelle to fuselage spine.
Allows access for maintenance and inspection of wing spar socket and internal components.
Heat from sun is dissipated through the wing surface.
Nacelle tilt mechanism moves wing spar to adjust flight control.
Electrical and data signals from fuselage to wing components.

requirements: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket
Wing spar socket, fuselage spine socket, payload bay socket
Pressurized avionics bay, high-altitude optimized props
Rotor thrust load transmitted through tilt axis to bearing seat
Servo torque transmitted through linkage to worm gear

verify.value: All required fields are present and correctly formatted.

mass_est_g

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
verify.rule: p0_feature_mapping

verify.value: All interfaces are reciprocal with neighboring subsystems.
verify.rule: interface_fit_class

verify.value: All critical joints have visible locking evidence and preload intent.
verify.rule: joint_evidence_rule

verify.value: Geometry_ops args.at coordinates are distributed across the local subsystem volume.
verify.rule: coordinate_distribution

verify.value: Uses a variety of feature types including drill, pocket, boss, channel, cylinder, chamfer, fillet, pattern, and engrave.
verify.rule: non_box_feature_variety

verify.value: Motion claims are grounded in a named mechanism type with its pivot/axis geometry present.
verify.rule: mechanism_named_not_implied

verify.value: Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible.
verify.rule: tilt_axis_readability

verify.value: Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable.
verify.rule: wing_fuselage_interface

verify.value: Output remains an educational mockup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details.
verify.rule: safe_mock_vtol_boundary

verify.value: Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, and motor/servo modules remain replaceable through access covers.
verify.rule: nacelle_load_and_service_path

verify.value: A primary datum feature (flat, bore, or pin) is explicitly named and placed.
verify.rule: primary_datum_evidence

verify.value: cad_brief includes ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step.
verify.rule: assembly_sequence_rule

verify.value: Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, pocket, drill, subtract. Subtractive features (drill/pocket/channel) and finishing (fillet/chamfer) both present.
verify.rule: expected_op_variety

verify.value: Dimensions match tiltrotor scale: 200-1500mm wingspan, 50-300mm fuselage dia. Wall thickness, hole diameters, fillet radii are physically plausible.
verify.rule: dimensional_realism
analysis_ready_rule

risk.desc: Potential for thermal expansion to affect tilt axis alignment.
risk.mit: Implement thermal expansion joints or use materials with similar thermal expansion coefficients.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for motor and ESC replacement.
risk.mit: Ensure access covers provide adequate clearance and are easily removable.

risk.desc: Possibility of servo linkage interference with other components.
risk.mit: Design clearance around servo linkage to avoid contact with adjacent parts.

risk.desc: Risk of torque transmission failure due to inadequate preload.
risk.mit: Verify preload values and ensure proper torque application during assembly.

risk.desc: Potential for misalignment during assembly due to lack of clear datum.
risk.mit: Establish clear primary datum feature and ensure all components align to it.
